questions.text: Where does the process of digestion start in humans?
questions.explanation: Digestion begins in the buccal cavity (mouth) where food is chewed and mixed with saliva.

questions.text: Bile juice is stored in a sac-like organ called the:
questions.explanation: Bile is produced by the liver and stored in the gall bladder until needed.

questions.text: The largest gland in the human body is the:
questions.explanation: The liver is a reddish-brown gland situated in the upper part of the abdomen on the right side.

questions.text: Amoeba captures its food with the help of:
questions.explanation: Amoeba pushes out finger-like projections called pseudopodia or 'false feet' to capture prey.

questions.text: Villi are finger-like outgrowths found in the inner walls of the:
questions.explanation: Villi increase the surface area for the absorption of digested food.

questions.text: The main function of the large intestine is:
questions.explanation: The large intestine absorbs water and some salts from the undigested food material.
Ruminants like cows swallow grass quickly and later bring it back to the mouth to chew it.
The salivary amylase enzyme in saliva acts on starch and converts it into simpler sugars.

questions.text: The acid secreted in the stomach to kill bacteria is:
questions.explanation: Hydrochloric acid (HCl) kills many bacteria and creates an acidic medium for protein digestion.

questions.text: Complete digestion of food takes place in the:
questions.explanation: The small intestine receives secretions and completes the digestion of all components of food.
